**mRNA-decapping enzyme 2** is a [protein](/wiki/Protein "Protein") that in humans is encoded by the *DCP2* [gene](/wiki/Gene "Gene").[[5]](#cite_note-pmid12218187-5)[[6]](#cite_note-pmid12417715-6)[[7]](#cite_note-entrez-7)

DCP2 is a key component of an mRNA-decapping complex required for removal of the 5-prime cap from mRNA prior to its degradation from the 5-prime end (Fenger-Gron et al., 2005).[supplied by OMIM][[7]](#cite_note-entrez-7)

## Interactions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DCP2&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Interactions")]

DCP2 has been shown to [interact](/wiki/Protein-protein_interaction "Protein-protein interaction") with [DCP1A](/wiki/DCP1A "DCP1A")[[8]](#cite_note-autogenerated1-8) and [UPF1](/wiki/UPF1 "UPF1").[[6]](#cite_note-pmid12417715-6)[[9]](#cite_note-pmid14527413-9)
